businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker transitioning into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning robotaxis, humanoid robots, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is rapidly transforming from an EV manufacturer into an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform company. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B, marking its first-ever annual revenue decline (-2.9%), with net income plunging 46.5%. Closed at $390.52 (-3.80%) on 3/5, hammered by collapsing China demand, tech selloff, and NHTSA D-3 uncertainty. Berlin Gigafactory works council election saw IG Metall fail to secure a majority, a positive outcome for Tesla. P/E 361x, Forward P/E 191x. Market cap $1.46T.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack utility-scale storage
Services/Other: ~13% of revenue ($12.5B). Supercharger network, insurance, maintenance
FSD subscription: $99/month autonomous driving software (subscription-only since Feb 2026)
keyProducts: Cybertruck (base AWD trim with massive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027)
Optimus (humanoid robot, Fremont factory conversion underway)
Megapack (utility-scale energy storage, Megapack 3 and Mega Block launching 2026)

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Slight decline from prior day but still extreme. Reflects AI/robotics premium

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects earnings recovery expectations but remains very elevated

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: First-ever FY decline. EV competition intensifying with price cuts

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Sharp decline from 7.24% in FY2024. Price cuts and R&D expansion

keyMetrics.name: Debt/Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Very healthy. Net cash position of $29.3B

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Declined vs FY2024. Profitability recovery needed

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD data deadline March 9 -- regulatory crossroads
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool: Tesla must submit FSD crash data to NHTSA by 3/9. Results could accelerate autonomous driving approval or trigger additional restrictions.

growthDrivers.title: German union IG Metall pushing for influence at Berlin Gigafactory
growthDrivers.description: Reuters: Germany's largest industrial union campaigning for breakthrough at Berlin Gigafactory works council elections.

growthDrivers.title: Cybertruck base AWD trim -- explosive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027
growthDrivers.description: New base trim generating massive demand, pushing expected delivery timelines to 2027.

growthDrivers.title: France & Norway market share rebounds in February -- European stabilization
growthDrivers.description: Reuters: Tesla regained market share in France and Norway in February, signaling stabilization after two years of European decline.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader, though overtaken globally by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy and 8.2B FSD miles are key moats.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly larger training dataset vs competitors.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infra (Supercharger).
NACS is North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Supercharger network.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales leader. Rapid European growth, US entry limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026 to compete directly with Model Y

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV outselling Model Y 2:1 in China

recentNews.headline: Tesla stock tumbles as China demand collapses amid tech selloff
recentNews.significance: China monthly deliveries down double digits MoM, wait times shrunk from months to 1-3 weeks

recentNews.headline: IG Metall fails to win majority in Berlin Gigafactory works council election
recentNews.significance: Secures Tesla's European management autonomy, eases cost increase concerns

recentNews.headline: Tesla tops 2026 supply chain sustainability rankings
recentNews.significance: Second consecutive year as ESG leader, strengthening institutional investor appeal

recentNews.headline: Nasdaq: Tesla's 2026 may hinge on March 9 outcome
recentNews.significance: NHTSA clearance could trigger rally; failure could mean 30% downside

recentNews.headline: ainvest: Tesla's March growth trajectory, assessing path to $450
recentNews.significance: Autonomous driving progress key condition for $450 breakout

analystOpinions.notableComment: Hold consensus among 45 analysts. Current price $390.52 now slightly below consensus $396. The March 9 NHTSA outcome will be the catalyst for target price revisions.

risks.title: Valuation risk at P/E 361x
risks.description: Extreme overvaluation vs current earnings. Significant correction risk if AI/robotics narrative fails.

risks.title: China demand collapse
risks.description: Monthly deliveries down double digits MoM, wait times shrunk to 1-3 weeks. Xiaomi SU7 and local competitors stealing share.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD regulatory risk (D-3)
risks.description: March 9 data submission results could halt autonomous driving program. Failure scenario implies 30% downside.

risks.title: Global EV competition intensifying
risks.description: BYD, Xiaomi, Rivian threatening on price competitiveness and product diversity.

risks.title: CEO risk
risks.description: Elon Musk's political statements accelerating consumer backlash in US and Europe, compounding subsidy cuts.

buyReasons.title: Now trading below analyst consensus $396
buyReasons.rationale: Dropped from $406 to $391, now below target. Short-term rebound potential.

buyReasons.title: Berlin union risk resolved
buyReasons.rationale: IG Metall failed to secure majority. European production cost increase fears eased.

buyReasons.title: FCF rebounded to $6.22B (+73.7% YoY)
buyReasons.rationale: Cash generation improving despite earnings decline. Investment capacity secured.

buyReasons.title: Energy business high growth + ESG No.1
buyReasons.rationale: Revenue $12.8B (+27% YoY). Supply chain sustainability top ranking strengthens institutional appeal.

overallOpinion: Collapsing China demand (double-digit MoM decline) and a broad tech selloff drove TSLA down 3.8% to $390, erasing the prior session's rebound in a single day.
The March 9 NHTSA FSD data deadline is now D-3. A pass could trigger a rally; a miss could mean 30% downside per Nasdaq's analysis.
Berlin union election loss by IG Metall is a positive, but declining US/Europe registrations and Musk's political baggage continue pressuring demand.
Testing 200-day SMA support at $388, RSI at 41 approaching oversold. A break below opens $350s; a bounce faces resistance at 50-day SMA $441.

Per Nasdaq: if NHTSA clears FSD on 3/9, expect a rally. If not, 30% downside is on the table. Stock is testing support at the 200-day moving average ($388). Below that, $350s come into play.

witty.hook: Tesla's queue in China just... disappeared
witty.text: $TSLA wait times went from 'months' to '1-3 weeks.' That's not a faster line, that's fewer customers. Down 3.8% to $390.52.
thread: Silver lining: IG Metall lost the Berlin factory union vote. So lines are disappearing everywhere. On the bright side, Tesla kept its No.1 supply chain sustainability crown for the second year.
The real showdown is 3 days out. March 9, NHTSA reviews FSD data. Nasdaq says failure means 30% downside. That's not a warning, that's a countdown.
Stock is clinging to its 200-day average at $388. At P/E 361x, if this support breaks, $350s are next. March 9 decides everything. investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-06

empathy.text: $TSLA down 3.8% to $390. Two days ago the bounce felt like relief. One session later, it's all gone. That stings.
thread: The China numbers hurt the most. Deliveries down double digits, wait times collapsed from months to 1-3 weeks. When the queue evaporates, it means buyers walked away.
At least Berlin went our way. IG Metall lost the union vote, so no cost explosion in Europe. And that ESG No.1 ranking? Still ours. Small wins.
But March 9 is the real test. NHTSA reviews FSD data. A pass changes everything. A fail could mean 30% more pain.

The 200-day average at $388 is right below us. If it holds, there's hope. If not, $350s. For long-term holders, maybe wait until after 3/9 to decide. Hang in there. investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-06
